name: Drifting
description: A vehicle intentionally exceeds normal tire grip and slides through a turn while retaining steerable control.
design_notes: Drifting depends on slip angle, tire grip curves, surface friction, counter-steer assist, and camera/audio cues. Designers should decide whether drift is a faster racing technique, a style reward, a boost charger, or a recovery tool before tuning vehicle handling.
design_purpose: Give players an expressive cornering technique that rewards timing and steering control.
Make low-traction surfaces and sharp turns feel skillful rather than purely punishing.
Support score, boost, style, or chase fantasy in vehicle-focused games.
player_fantasy: The player feels stylish and in control while sliding at the edge of traction.

parameters.name: drift_angle_threshold
parameters.description: Slip angle required before the vehicle enters drift handling or scoring state.

parameters.name: tire_grip_curve_id
parameters.description: Grip curve used while lateral slip increases and recovers.

parameters.name: countersteer_assist_strength
parameters.description: Assist strength that helps align steering with recoverable drift direction.

parameters.name: drift_charge_per_degree
parameters.description: Optional boost or score gain accumulated from sustained drift angle.

parameters.name: surface_grip_modifier_id
parameters.description: Surface modifier that changes drift initiation and recovery behavior.

edge_cases: The vehicle starts drifting while airborne or with partial wheel contact.
A collision occurs during drift and should cancel, preserve, or convert drift charge.
Surface changes mid-corner and grip recovery becomes abrupt.
Client prediction detects drift while server contact state says the vehicle is not grounded.
common_bugs: Drift state toggles rapidly around the threshold and flickers camera, score, or boost UI.
Countersteer assist fights player input and prevents intentional spin recovery.
Drift charge continues while sliding backward or scraping a wall.
Audio and tire smoke play from visual slip while physics state has already recovered.
balancing_notes: If drifting grants boost, normal cornering still needs a reason to exist on some track shapes.
Higher assist improves accessibility but can flatten mastery unless optional or difficulty-scaled.
Different surfaces should alter drift feel predictably rather than randomly reducing control.
accessibility_notes: Offer steer assist, reduced camera yaw, and optional drift automation for players who struggle with counter-steering.
Use visual, audio, and controller cues to indicate drift state without relying only on tire smoke.

implementation_notes.unity: Compute slip angle from Rigidbody velocity and vehicle forward vector in FixedUpdate, then switch tire_grip_curve_id or lateral friction parameters. Use hysteresis around drift_angle_threshold and store drift state in rollback snapshots.
implementation_notes.godot: Custom VehicleBody3D or CharacterBody vehicle controllers should derive drift from local velocity and wheel contact. Apply countersteer assist after player steering input but before integration, and debug draw slip angle.
implementation_notes.unreal: Chaos Vehicles can expose tire slip; gameplay code should gate drift scoring from authoritative grounded contact and validated driver input. Replicate drift state or score events rather than raw tire telemetry.
implementation_notes.web: For arcade browser vehicles, compute local lateral velocity and slip angle in a fixed-step model. Keep camera shake, tire marks, and audio subscribed to committed drift state so prediction corrections do not leave stale effects.

relationships.reason: Drifting needs throttle, steering, traction, and velocity state from vehicle movement.
Brake input or handbrake state commonly initiates controlled drift angle.
Subtle camera and audio feedback can reinforce tire slip without changing traction rules.

example_games.title: Mario Kart 8
example_games.note: Uses drifting as a cornering and boost-building mechanic.

example_games.title: Rocket League
example_games.note: Uses powerslide-style drifting to recover, turn sharply, and reposition around the ball.

example_games.title: Need for Speed: Hot Pursuit
example_games.note: Uses arcade drifting for pursuit and racing feel.
